content.text:
Both files updated. The fix:

- **Toolbar.jsx** — `toggleLink()` now uses `editor.update()` + `$toggleLink('https://')` directly, bypassing the `validateUrl` check that was rejecting the placeholder URL
- **FloatingSelectionToolbar.jsx** — Same change

The `$toggleLink` function creates the link node directly without going through `LinkPlugin`'s `validateUrl`, so `'https://'` as a placeholder works. The FloatingLinkEditor will then let users edit the actual URL.

content.thinking:
So the issue is that on desktop the toolbar wraps to multiple rows (`flex-wrap: wrap`), but on mobile it switches to `flex-wrap: nowrap` with `overflow-x: auto`. This means all buttons go into a single scrollable row, and if the user can't tell they can scroll, or the scroll isn't working well, they only see the first few buttons.

But the user says "toolbar rows are cut off" - so maybe on mobile with `nowrap` + `overflow-x: auto`, the overflow isn't actually scrolling? Or maybe the parent container is cutting it off?

Wait - the `.toolbar-wrapper` doesn't have `overflow: hidden` explicitly, but it also doesn't have `overflow: visible`. And on mobile it gets `position: relative`. 

I think the real problem is that on mobile, the user expects to see all the buttons wrapped across multiple rows like on desktop, but instead they're getting a single scrollable row with no visual indicator that scrolling is possible—just a subtle gradient hint on the right edge that most people won't notice.

The solution is to keep `flex-wrap: wrap` enabled on mobile too, so all buttons display in multiple rows instead of trying to hide them behind a hidden scrollbar.

content.text:
Got it. On desktop the toolbar wraps to multiple rows, but on mobile it switches to `flex-wrap: nowrap` with horizontal scroll (and the scrollbar is hidden). So you only see the first row's worth of buttons with no obvious way to reach the rest.

The simplest fix: keep `flex-wrap: wrap` on mobile too so all buttons are visible in rows, just like desktop.

message.content: i want it to be scrollable instead of showing them all

content.content:
1→# Fix: Link Button Not Working
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→The link toolbar button (and floating toolbar link button) silently fail. Root cause: when creating a new link, the Toolbar dispatches `TOGGLE_LINK_COMMAND` with payload `'https://'`. The `LinkPlugin` handler calls `validateUrl('https://')`, which runs `new URL('https://')` — this **throws** because `https://` alone is not a valid URL (no hostname). So `validateUrl` returns `false`, the handler returns `false`, and the link is never created.
     6→
     7→Same issue exists in `FloatingSelectionToolbar.jsx` which also dispatches `TOGGLE_LINK_COMMAND` with `'https://'`.
     8→
     9→## Root Cause
    10→
    11→**File:** `Toolbar.jsx:379` and `FloatingSelectionToolbar.jsx:103`
    12→```js
    13→editor.dispatchCommand(TOGGLE_LINK_COMMAND, 'https://');
    14→// ↑ LinkPlugin calls validateUrl('https://') → new URL('https://') → THROWS → returns false
    15→```
    16→
    17→**File:** `RichTextEditor.jsx:290`, `ChapterEditor.jsx:204`, `editJournal.jsx`
    18→```jsx
    19→<LinkPlugin validateUrl={(url) => {
    20→    try { return Boolean(new URL(url)); } catch { return false; }
    21→}} />
    22→// ↑ 'https://' has no hostname, so new URL() throws, returns false
    23→```
    24→
    25→## Fix
    26→
    27→Two-part fix — both are needed:
    28→
    29→### 1. Toolbar.jsx (line ~379) — Use `$toggleLink` directly instead of dispatching command with placeholder URL
    30→
    31→Replace the `toggleLink` function to call `$toggleLink` inside `editor.update()` which bypasses the `validateUrl` check for the initial creation. The FloatingLinkEditor will handle actual URL editing.
    32→
    33→```js
    34→const toggleLink = () => {
    35→    editor.update(() => {
    36→        const selection = $getSelection();
    37→        if (!$isRangeSelection(selection)) return;
    38→        if (activeStates.isLink) {
    39→            $toggleLink(null);          // remove link
    40→        } else {
    41→            $toggleLink('https://');    // create link with placeholder
    42→        }
    43→    });
    44→};
    45→```
    46→
    47→Requires adding import: `$toggleLink` from `@lexical/link`
    48→
    49→### 2. FloatingSelectionToolbar.jsx (line ~103) — Same fix
    50→
    51→Replace:
    52→```js
    53→editor.dispatchCommand(TOGGLE_LINK_COMMAND, 'https://');
    54→```
    55→With direct `editor.update()` + `$toggleLink('https://')` call.
    56→
    57→Requires adding import: `$toggleLink` from `@lexical/link`
    58→
    59→### Files to modify
    60→| File | Change |
    61→|---|---|
    62→| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/Toolbar.jsx` | Import `$toggleLink`, rewrite `toggleLink()` to use `editor.update()` |
    63→| `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/plugins/FloatingSelectionToolbar.jsx` | Import `$toggleLink`, rewrite `toggleLink()` to use `editor.update()` |
    64→
    65→## Verification
    66→1. Open editor, select text, click link button → link should be created with `https://` placeholder
    67→2. FloatingLinkEditor should appear showing the URL for editing
    68→3. Click link button again on linked text → link should be removed
    69→4. Select text in floating selection toolbar, click link → same behavior
    70→5. Verify auto-link still works (type a full URL like `https://google.com` → auto-linked)
    71→

content.content: The file C:\Users\johnm\.claude\plans\splendid-churning-candle.md has been updated successfully.

lines: -The link toolbar button (and floating toolbar link button) silently fail. Root cause: when creating a new link, the Toolbar dispatches `TOGGLE_LINK_COMMAND` with payload `'https://'`. The `LinkPlugin` handler calls `validateUrl('https://')`, which runs `new URL('https://')` — this **throws** because `https://` alone is not a valid URL (no hostname). So `validateUrl` returns `false`, the handler returns `false`, and the link is never created.
-Same issue exists in `FloatingSelectionToolbar.jsx` which also dispatches `TOGGLE_LINK_COMMAND` with `'https://'`.
-// ↑ LinkPlugin calls validateUrl('https://') → new URL('https://') → THROWS → returns false
-    try { return Boolean(new URL(url)); } catch { return false; }
-// ↑ 'https://' has no hostname, so new URL() throws, returns false
-### 1. Toolbar.jsx (line ~379) — Use `$toggleLink` directly instead of dispatching command with placeholder URL

-Replace the `toggleLink` function to call `$toggleLink` inside `editor.update()` which bypasses the `validateUrl` check for the initial creation. The FloatingLinkEditor will handle actual URL editing.
-1. Open editor, select text, click link button → link should be created with `https://` placeholder
-2. FloatingLinkEditor should appear showing the URL for editing
-3. Click link button again on linked text → link should be removed
-4. Select text in floating selection toolbar, click link → same behavior
-5. Verify auto-link still works (type a full URL like `https://google.com` → auto-linked)

+The editor toolbar wraps to multiple rows on mobile, taking up too much vertical space. The user wants it to be a single scrollable row instead. A previous attempt used hidden scrollbars which made it non-discoverable — this time we'll keep a visible thin scrollbar so users know they can scroll.
+    background: linear-gradient(to right, transparent, var(--editor-bg, var(--bg-elevated)));
+Key differences from the old broken version:
+- **Visible thin scrollbar** (`scrollbar-width: thin` + 3px webkit scrollbar) instead of hidden — users can see there's more content
+- **Fade gradient** on right edge as additional visual hint
+1. Open editor on mobile (or use browser devtools responsive mode at ≤640px)
+2. Toolbar should be a single row with a thin scrollbar visible at bottom
+3. Swiping/scrolling reveals all buttons including alignment and colors on the right
+4. Right edge has subtle fade gradient hinting at more content
+5. Desktop (>640px) remains unchanged — toolbar wraps normally
